title: Well Test
description: Test the village well by memorizing pressure beats from its four pipeheads and tapping them back before the pump stalls.

howToPlayJson.title: Well Test
howToPlayJson.objective: Replay increasingly complex water-pulse sequences on a ring of four touch pipeheads, react to rotating test rules, and build the highest combo before three failed inspections end the run.

controls.outcome: Inputs that pipehead into the current sequence

controls.input: Press and hold a pipehead briefly
controls.outcome: Matches rare long-tone beats later in the run for bonus score

controls.input: Tap the center well cap once per run
controls.outcome: Replays the last two beats but halves the current multiplier

steps: A round starts with the center well thumping a short musical pulse pattern through 4 pipeheads: north, east, south, west.
The player watches and listens, then taps the same pipeheads back in order on a mobile-first circular layout.
Every 2 rounds, the well announces a test condition with a sharp audio sting and icon flash: replay in reverse, ignore the cracked pipe, or
If the player adapts correctly, the sequence length grows by 1 beat and the tempo tightens slightly.
Perfect rounds add to a pressure combo that multiplies score; using the wrong pipe, missing a hold note, or obeying the wrong test rule rupt
After 3 ruptures, the pump fails, the session ends, and the score can be posted to a simple daily/offline-best leaderboard.
howToPlayJson.scoring: Score 100 points per correct beat, +50 per perfect round, and apply a pressure multiplier that starts at x1 and rises by +0.5 every two flawless rounds up to x5. Any mistake breaks the combo. Reverse-rule and cracked-pip
tips: Make each pipehead a distinct instrument tone so players can learn by ear as much as by sight.
Keep runs to about 3 minutes by starting at 3 beats and capping most sessions around 10-12 beats.
Use strong haptic taps and bassy pump sounds for success, and a sharp dry clank for failures.
Store daily best and local-device all-time best so the leaderboard works offline and sync can stay optional.
